General Rules Of Interpretation.name: GRI 1
General Rules Of Interpretation.rule: If the description and any relative section or chapter notes make a candidate the undisputable best fit for the Item Description, choose it. If more than one could be suitable, proceed to GRI 3(a)
General Rules Of Interpretation.exception: If none of the candidates accurately describe the item or meaningfully progress the Selection Path, and 'Other' is an available candidate, choose it. Otherwise, proceed to GRI 3(a)

General Rules Of Interpretation.name: GRI 2(a)
General Rules Of Interpretation.rule: Any reference in a candidate to an article shall be taken to include a reference to that article incomplete, unfinished, or unassembled, provided that, as entered, the article has the essential character of the complete, finished, or assembled article.

General Rules Of Interpretation.name: GRI 2(b)
General Rules Of Interpretation.rule: Any reference in a candidate to a material or substance shall be taken to include a reference to mixtures or combinations of that material or substance with other materials or substances. Any reference to goods of a given material or substance shall be taken to include a reference to goods consisting wholly or partly of such material or substance. Goods consisting of more than one material or substance shall be classified according to the principles of rule 3.

General Rules Of Interpretation.name: GRI 3(a)
General Rules Of Interpretation.rule: Choose the candidate that most specifically describes the item. If no candidate is most specific or the item is primarily a mixed or composite good or a retail set, mention this and go to GRI 3(b) to continue classification.

General Rules Of Interpretation.name: GRI 3(b)
General Rules Of Interpretation.rule: Identify which aspect of the item most clearly controls the items identity, such as primary function, dominant material, intended use, or monetary value. Choose the candidate that most closely matches the controlling aspect. If no aspect seems to control the items identity, mention this and go to GRI 3(c) to continue classification.

General Rules Of Interpretation.name: GRI 3(c)
General Rules Of Interpretation.rule: Among the candidates that have equal merit based your analysis of 3(a) and 3(b), choose the candidate that occurs last in numerical order.
